'''Liam Horne''' (born 3 February 1998) is a [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], and for the [[Norths Devils]] and [[Central Queensland Capras]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].

==Early life==
Horne was born in [[Port Moresby]], [[Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{cite web|date=23 June 2022|title=Meet the PNG Kumuls debutant, Liam Horne|url=https://pnghausbung.com/meet-the-png-kumuls-debutants-liam-horne/|work=Papua New Guinea Today|access-date=26 July 2023}}</ref>

==Playing career==
=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 24 July 2023, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Horne on a one-and-a-half-year deal, with an option for a further year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 July 2023 |title=Liam Horne: Castleford Tigers sign versatile Papua New Guinea forward |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66295609 |access-date=9 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=24 July 2023 |title=Papua New Guinea international makes immediate Super League switch in bid for club's top flight survival |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/papua-new-guinea-international-makes-immediate-super-league-switch-in-bid-for-clubs-top-flight-survival |access-date=9 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=24 July 2023 |title=Horne joins the Fords |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8691 |access-date=9 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He made his [[Super League]] debut for the Tigers on 4 August against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 August 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford 0-28 Huddersfield - Sam Halsall hat-trick as Giants win fourth in a row |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66397936 |access-date=9 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> His performance against [[Wakefield Trinity]] in his second game for Castleford saw him voted Fans' Man of the Match and named in the Super League Team of the Week.<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1693578701299130866|title=A superb display from Liam Horne on his first Tigers start to win our @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match from Friday night!}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=20 August 2023 |title=Super League Team of the Week: Castleford Tigers, Wigan Warriors, St Helens, Leeds Rhinos and Catalans Dragons dominate |url=https://www.totalrl.com/super-league-team-of-the-week-castleford-tigers-wigan-warriors-st-helens-leeds-rhinos-and-catalans-dragons-dominate/ |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet|author=Betfred Super League|author-link=Super League|user=SuperLeague|number=1693615991102546191|title=The Round 22 #SuperLeague Team of the Week}}</ref>

Horne was assigned squad number 14 ahead of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=20 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers 2024 squad numbers: 11 new signings receive their jerseys, with 31 shirts handed out |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2024-squad-numbers-11-new-signings-receive-their-jerseys-with-31-shirts-handed-out |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He was voted Fans' Man of the Match in the round 1 defeat against the [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1759552352132936056|title=The @HuntersHomes Man of the match goes to Liam Horne!}}</ref> and scored his first Castleford try in round 2 against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 February 2024 |title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 26-22 Castleford Tigers - Hosts hold on for first win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337686 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In May, Horne signed a two-year contract extension with the Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web |date=30 May 2024 |title=Castleford forward Horne commits future until 2026 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/crgg7wknrlno |access-date=24 July 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 18, he scored two tries and was voted Man of the Match in a win against [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 July 2024 |title=Castleford defy Catalans fightback with late Hoy try |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cg3e53lvjw8o |access-date=24 July 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1815747561006698750|title=He's won it again! The @HuntersHomes Man of the Match goes to Liam Horne!}}</ref> At Castleford's end-of-season awards, Horne was named Players' Player of the Year.<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1832757274068050172|title=Last nights Players Player of the Year, Liam Horne!}}</ref>

=== Leigh Leopards ===
On 24 October 2025, Horne signed for [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]] having been released from Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 October 2025 |title=Leigh sign Castleford forward Horne |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4g394mlekvo |access-date=24 October 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

==Representative==
Horne made his international debut for [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in their 24–14 victory over [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in the 2022 Pacific Test.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://northsdevilsrlfc.com/2022/06/20/horne-lives-dream-with-kumuls-selection/ | title=Horne lives dream with Kumuls selection | date=20 June 2022 }}</ref>

On 12 October 2025 he played for the [[PNG Prime Minister's XIII]] in the 28-10 defeat to Australia’s [[Prime Minister's XIII]] in Port Moresby<ref>{{cite web|website=FoxSports.au|date=12 Oct 2025|author=Daniel Nuttall|title=Zero for seven?! Aussie’s double hat-trick powers PM’s XII past PNG despite horrific kicking|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/rugby-league-2025-aus-pm-xiii-v-png-pm-xiii-teams-where-to-watch-start-time-kevin-walters-mitch-moses-rugby-league-news/news-story/b7b5454aadeb76356a021e7e9bbcefa3}}</ref>
